parsed_review.summary: CP00-903 is a descriptor-only RP30 embedded-HRX bridge pack (risk A, 10 units, RP30.P01.M06.S04-RP30.P01.M08.S03; 2 Employee tail + 5 EmploymentProfile + 3 HRDocument implementation rows). Read-only inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms the pack-level descriptor-verification claim is fully supported and the no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data boundary holds. Implementation (packages/hrx/src/registry.js createHrxCp903EmployeeProfileDocumentBridgeDescriptor + createHrxDomainRows, lines 529-577 and 1465-1598) generates deep-frozen rows with all runtime/write/real-data/secret/document-body/storage-runtime flags false, and spreads HRX_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (all-false) into the descriptor. validators.js (validateHrxCp903...Coverage/Descriptor, lines 965-1130, and validateHrxPeopleContract 1132-1156) enforce Employee/User separation (reverse-link only, user_account_conflated=false, may_authorize_user_session=false), EmploymentProfile->Employee-only linkage (references_user_account=false), and HRDocument->EmploymentProfile-only linkage with document body/storage runtime closed, plus payroll-runtime/HR-AI-final-judgment/permission-write/audit-write/runtime-receipt/separate-product closure. Grep found no writeFile/appendFile/child_process/exec/spawn/fetch/http/process.env/eval/.write in packages/hrx/src; scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs is read-only (readFile + console + process.exit, no writes). No real PII/secrets in source or contract (real-data grep matched only boolean attestation fields like credential_or_secret_included:false). Tests (test/model.test.js 335-398) assert CP903 binding, coverage distribution {implementation:10; micro Employee:2/EmploymentProfile:5/HRDocument:3}, descriptor boundaries, and contract projection, and trace-pass against the live plan and generated contract. Plan (docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-903 entry), manifest plan_binding_snapshot, contracts/hrx-people-contract.json (latest_pack CP00-903, latest_projection HrxCp903EmployeeProfileDocumentBridgeDescriptor, validation.valid=true, top-level no_write_attestation all-false, required_capabilities/safety_gates/no_leak_guards present), README, and prompt distributions are mutually consistent. Changed-file-scope correctly classifies the HRX files as active implementation refs and treats progress-control-room/workbook/docx files as unrelated dirty/untracked (not pack scope). Authority boundary holds: descriptor authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, claude_packet.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-904; Hermes packet emits_runtime_receipt=false (deterministic review-evidence only, not embedded product runtime). CP178+ evidence hardening satisfied: readiness-report.json auth_preflight passed (exit 0, 1449-byte valid-JSON stdout, stderr 0 bytes, no auth failure / 0-byte / malformed / fenced-prose / tool-call-shaped / missing-field output). Provenance: this verdict is based solely on read-only source inspection; I did NOT re-execute the validation ladder (npm test / npm run validate / closeout-pack:validate / git diff --check, etc.) -- command-evidence.json currently records the initial HRX validation subset (syntax check, model test, rp30:hrx/rp30/hrx:requirements/hrx:weighted validate, all exit 0) with the full ladder deferred to post-review normalization per the pack's own note, so those ladder exit codes are pipeline-claimed and not independently re-run here. The manifest honestly reports production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false pre-review and defers runtime/enterprise-trust opening to human approval at CP00-904 or later; the pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim, so Claude remaining a read-only non-final reviewer does not block this CP closeout (blocks_goal_closeout interpreted as this CP closeout goal only). No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
